headline: Meta grants senior executives stock awards to retain talent
summary: Meta Platforms on Tuesday granted ‌restricted stock units and ‌stock options for some of ​its senior executives, including CFO Susan Li and Chief Technology Officer Andrew Bosworth, ‌tying them ⁠to performance and aggressive share price targets ⁠as it looks to retain top talent. Stock awards ​are typically ​issued ​to reward employees, ‌retain top talent, and align their interests with the company's long-term success. A Meta spokesperson said the pay ‌packages represent a "big ​bet" and ​that they "will ​not be realized ‌unless Meta achieves ​massive future ​success, benefiting all of our shareholders".

content.title: Meta grants senior executives stock awards to retain talent
content.summary: Meta Platforms on Tuesday granted ‌restricted stock units and ‌stock options for some of ​its senior executives, including CFO Susan Li and Chief Technology Officer Andrew Bosworth, ‌tying them ⁠to performance and aggressive share price targets ⁠as it looks to retain top talent. Stock awards ​are typically ​issued ​to reward employees, ‌retain top talent, and align their interests with the company's long-term success. A Meta spokesperson said the pay ‌packages represent a "big ​bet" and ​that they "will ​not be realized ‌unless Meta achieves ​massive future ​success, benefiting all of our shareholders".

Meta Platforms Inc. will be the first major customer for the company’s chip, called an AGI CPU, Arm said Tuesday at an event in San Francisco. headline: Meta boosts top executives' pay with stock options as AI race heats up
summary: Meta Platforms is increasing compensation for top executives, including its first-ever offer of stock options, as it tries ‌to fend off competition in the artificial intelligence race and incentivize leaders to ‌stay with the company for several years.  The pay boost comes as Meta, like other major tech firms, races to ​gain an edge in AI, intensifying a battle for senior talent.  Chief Financial Officer Susan Li, Chief Technology Officer Andrew Bosworth, Chief Product Officer Chris Cox, Chief Operating Officer Javier Olivan, President Dina Powell ‌McCormick and Chief Legal Officer Curtis ⁠Mahoney are eligible for the stock options, regulatory filings late on Tuesday showed.

content.title: Meta boosts top executives' pay with stock options as AI race heats up
content.summary: Meta Platforms is increasing compensation for top executives, including its first-ever offer of stock options, as it tries ‌to fend off competition in the artificial intelligence race and incentivize leaders to ‌stay with the company for several years.  The pay boost comes as Meta, like other major tech firms, races to ​gain an edge in AI, intensifying a battle for senior talent.  Chief Financial Officer Susan Li, Chief Technology Officer Andrew Bosworth, Chief Product Officer Chris Cox, Chief Operating Officer Javier Olivan, President Dina Powell ‌McCormick and Chief Legal Officer Curtis ⁠Mahoney are eligible for the stock options, regulatory filings late on Tuesday showed.

headline: China bars Manus co-founders from leaving country amid Meta deal review, FT reports
summary: China has barred two co-founders of artificial intelligence startup Manus from leaving the country as ‌regulators review whether Meta's $2 billion acquisition of the ‌firm violated investment rules, the Financial Times reported.  Manus's chief executive Xiao Hong and ​chief scientist Ji Yichao were summoned to a meeting in Beijing with the National Development and Reform Commission (NDRC) this month, the FT said on Wednesday, citing people with knowledge of the ‌matter.  Following the meeting, the ⁠executives were told they could not leave China due to a regulatory review, though they are ⁠free to travel within the country, the report said.

content.title: China bars Manus co-founders from leaving country amid Meta deal review, FT reports
content.summary: China has barred two co-founders of artificial intelligence startup Manus from leaving the country as ‌regulators review whether Meta's $2 billion acquisition of the ‌firm violated investment rules, the Financial Times reported.  Manus's chief executive Xiao Hong and ​chief scientist Ji Yichao were summoned to a meeting in Beijing with the National Development and Reform Commission (NDRC) this month, the FT said on Wednesday, citing people with knowledge of the ‌matter.  Following the meeting, the ⁠executives were told they could not leave China due to a regulatory review, though they are ⁠free to travel within the country, the report said.

headline: Arm AGI CPU Debut Puts AI Data Center Ambitions In Focus
summary: Arm Holdings (NasdaqGS:ARM) has introduced its first production silicon, the Arm AGI CPU, aimed at AI data centers. The launch marks a move beyond Arm's traditional IP licensing model into fully designed data center hardware. Meta and several large industry players are involved as launch partners and early co developers for the new CPU. For years, Arm has focused on licensing its chip architectures, which power a wide range of smartphones, embedded devices, and increasingly, data center...

headline: 1 Software Stock for Long-Term Investors and 2 We Turn Down
summary: Software is eating the world, and virtually no business is left untouched by it. This secular theme makes SaaS companies attractive investment candidates but also comes with higher valuations that cause volatility. Unfortunately, the rich prices have haunted them over the past six months as the industry has shed 25.2%. This drop was especially disheartening since the S&P 500 held its ground.

content.title: 1 Software Stock for Long-Term Investors and 2 We Turn Down
content.summary: Software is eating the world, and virtually no business is left untouched by it. This secular theme makes SaaS companies attractive investment candidates but also comes with higher valuations that cause volatility. Unfortunately, the rich prices have haunted them over the past six months as the industry has shed 25.2%. This drop was especially disheartening since the S&P 500 held its ground.

headline: Meta Targets $9 Trillion Value With AI-Driven Exec Pay Plan That Outstrips Musk and Tesla
summary: Meta Platforms  has unveiled ambitious executive pay incentives, targeting a $9 trillion market capitalization by 2031 as it looks to get ahead in the intensifying AI talent race.  Under the stock options program, which includes six leaders but not CEO Mark Zuckerberg, the full value of the options would only be realized if Meta stock jumps more than 500% to $3,727, according to filings late Tuesday.  Tesla  did something similar when it proposed a pay package worth up to $1 trillion for CEO Elon Musk—if a series of ambitious targets are met over the next decade, including boost the electric-vehicle maker to an $8.5 trillion valuation.

content.title: Meta Targets $9 Trillion Value With AI-Driven Exec Pay Plan That Outstrips Musk and Tesla
content.summary: Meta Platforms  has unveiled ambitious executive pay incentives, targeting a $9 trillion market capitalization by 2031 as it looks to get ahead in the intensifying AI talent race.  Under the stock options program, which includes six leaders but not CEO Mark Zuckerberg, the full value of the options would only be realized if Meta stock jumps more than 500% to $3,727, according to filings late Tuesday.  Tesla  did something similar when it proposed a pay package worth up to $1 trillion for CEO Elon Musk—if a series of ambitious targets are met over the next decade, including boost the electric-vehicle maker to an $8.5 trillion valuation.

summary: (Bloomberg) -- Meta Platforms Inc.’s rollout of new display-equipped Ray-Ban smart glasses in the European Union has been hampered by battery and artificial intelligence regulations in addition to supply constraints. The social networking giant wants to launch the product in the EU with partner EssilorLuxottica SA, but has been unable to secure enough supply, according to a person with knowledge of the situation. headline: Nvidia Stock Is Rising. Why Arm’s New AI Chip Is Not a Threat.
summary: Nvidia  stock was rising early Wednesday as the chip maker’s shares looked past the entry of  Arm Holdings  into the artificial-intelligence processor market.  Nvidia has been the dominant AI chipmaker for a while now, but new competitors keep arriving.  Arm, which had previously licensed its designs for chips, announced a new data center central-processing unit (CPU) on Tuesday, its first foray into making its own semiconductors.

Arm, which had previously licensed its designs for chips, announced a new data center central-processing unit (CPU) on Tuesday, its first foray into making its own semiconductors.

headline: Meta Aims to Be a $9 Trillion Stock, New Executive Pay Package Shows. It’s Trying to Out-Tesla Tesla.
summary: Meta Platforms  has unveiled ambitious executive pay incentives, targeting a $9 trillion market capitalization by 2031 as it looks to get ahead in the intensifying AI talent race.  Under the stock options program, which includes six leaders but not CEO Mark Zuckerberg, the full value of the options would only be realized if Meta stock jumps more than 500% to $3,727, according to filings late Tuesday.  Meta stock rose 1.4% ahead of the open Wednesday.

headline: UiPath Chief Technology Officer Raghu Malpani Takes Expanded Role as Chief Product and Technology Officer
summary: NEW YORK, March 25, 2026--UiPath (NYSE: PATH), a global leader in agentic automation, today announced that Chief Technology Officer Raghu Malpani will be taking on an expanded role as Chief Product and Technology Officer. Malpani will lead the company’s product and engineering teams and will continue to report to UiPath CEO and Executive Chairman Daniel Dines. This expanded role is effective March 25, 2026.

headline: Arm Rises on Plan to Sell Own Chips With Meta as Key Client
summary: (Bloomberg) -- Shares of Arm Holdings Plc, which made its name licensing technology to semiconductor makers, rose by the most in almost a year after it said it will sell its own chips for the first time — a move forecast to generate about $15 billion annually within five years. Meta Platforms Inc. will be the first major customer for the company’s chip, called an AGI CPU, Arm said Tuesday at an event in San Francisco. The product will have as many as 136 cores — a measure of processing power — a

headline: These Stocks Are Today’s Movers: Arm, Meta, Alibaba, JD.com, Chewy, DigitalOcean, GameStop, and More
summary: Stocks were rising Wednesday as investors hoped for progress in cease-fire talks between the U.S. and Iran and a swift end to the war in the Middle East.  While shares initially fell Tuesday when the company unveiled its plans to enter the market for data-center chips, Arm laid out a series of aggressive sales targets for the coming years in after-hours trading, boosting shares.  Investors are hopeful a swift end to the war in the Middle East would weaken the case for higher interest rates.

headline: Leaders of AI Firm Bought by Meta Are Restricted From Leaving China
summary: SINGAPORE—China has told two co-founders of artificial-intelligence startup Manus not to leave the country while authorities review the company’s $2.5 billion sale to Meta Platforms, people familiar with the matter said.  Earlier this month, Manus’s  Xiao Hong  and Ji Yichao were called in for a meeting with officials from the National Development and Reform Commission in Beijing to discuss the acquisition, the people said.  Officials later told the two Singapore-based executives not to leave China until receiving further instructions because the review is ongoing, the people said, describing the message as guidance rather than a formal exit ban.

content.title: Leaders of AI Firm Bought by Meta Are Restricted From Leaving China
content.summary: SINGAPORE—China has told two co-founders of artificial-intelligence startup Manus not to leave the country while authorities review the company’s $2.5 billion sale to Meta Platforms, people familiar with the matter said.  Earlier this month, Manus’s  Xiao Hong  and Ji Yichao were called in for a meeting with officials from the National Development and Reform Commission in Beijing to discuss the acquisition, the people said.  Officials later told the two Singapore-based executives not to leave China until receiving further instructions because the review is ongoing, the people said, describing the message as guidance rather than a formal exit ban.
